---
title: "About"
lang: en
---


My name is **Aurélien Nicosia**.
I am a lecturer in mathematics and statistics at the Department of Mathematics and Statistics at Université Laval (Québec). I am also the coordinator of the [Center for Tutoring and Learning (CDA)](https://www.fsg.ulaval.ca/departements/departement-de-mathematiques-et-de-statistique/etudier/centre-de-depannage-et-dapprentissage), a student support space where students receive help each term.

My work sits at the intersection of university pedagogy, applied statistics, and the development of digital tools for teaching and research.
I place strong emphasis on reproducibility, open science, and knowledge accessibility.

---

### Recent highlights

- Development of [GPT-CDA](cda/index.qmd), featured in [ULaval News on May 22, 2026](https://nouvelles.ulaval.ca/2026/05/22/le-gpt-cda-un-robot-conversationnel-qui-epaule-les-etudiantes-et-etudiants-dans-les-cours-de-mathematiques-et-de-statistique-66c35c61-4649-41a0-befb-c2f79036ac30).
- Publication of [*Beyond the next step: A multi-criteria generative validation framework for step selection functions*](https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.70313) in *Methods in Ecology and Evolution*.
- Release of [`DonutMap`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/DonutMap/index.html) on CRAN in 2026, with a [dedicated page](packages/donutmap.qmd) and open documentation.
- Release of [`ggcircular`](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/ggcircular/index.html) on CRAN in 2026, with open documentation on [GitHub](https://github.com/AurelienNicosiaULaval/ggcircular).
- Workshop on AI and teaching at SSC 2026, with a [public teaching-resource gallery](https://aureliennicosiaulaval.github.io/ssc-2026-ai-teaching-gallery/).
- 2026 New Investigator Presentation Award at SSC 2026 for a presentation on LOO-LBFP.

---

### Interests

- **University pedagogy**: active learning, formative assessment, and design of interactive resources.
- **Applied statistics**: multi-state modeling, density estimation, directional data.
- **Digital tools**: R, Python, Quarto, GitHub, Shiny, and responsible use of AI in education.
- **Innovation**: support for learning, interdisciplinary collaboration, and sharing francophone educational resources.

---

### Location and contact

📍 Québec, QC, Canada
🏛️ Université Laval - Department of Mathematics and Statistics
✉️ [aurelien.nicosia@mat.ulaval.ca](mailto:aurelien.nicosia@mat.ulaval.ca)

---

### About this site

This website is built with [**Quarto**](https://quarto.org) and deployed automatically with **GitHub Pages**.
It gathers my activities in **research**, **teaching**, and **pedagogical innovation**.
The source code is available in the repository linked in the navigation bar.

💡 If you want to suggest a correction or improvement, click the pencil (*Edit*) icon at the top of the page to open the corresponding version in GitHub.
